[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2018-10-19
[TITLE]: Yet Another Ukrainian Air Force Su-25 Insane Low Pass Video Emerges - The Aviationist
[METADATA/INTRO]: Pair of Su-25M1s flying ultra-low altitude in a new video. It’s pretty obvious: Ukrainian Air Force pilots fly low quite often and new videos showing a Ukrainian jet buzzing someone at airbases around the country appear regularly....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: The latest one is a footage showing two Su-25M1 flying low over a taxiway. It’s not clear when it was filmed nor where. However, considered the number of Il-76MD cargo aircraft that you can see in the clip, it could well be Melitopol airbase, in southeastern Ukraine, home of the 25th Transport Aviation Brigade. The Su-25M1 Frogfoot jets were probably operating out of Mykolaiv/Kulbakino airbase, to the west of Melitopol, home of the 299th Tactical Aviation Brigade. 
 Low pass of two 🇺🇦#Ukrainian Sukhoi Su-25 Frogfoot jet! pic.twitter.com/zqRGcq85Vy 
 — Capt(N) (@Capt_Navy) October 15, 2018 
 As a side note, as already reported here at The Aviationist, the Ukrainian Air Force in currently supporting Ex. Clear Sky 2018. During the drills, the service suffered the loss of a Su-27UB1M “70 Blue” on Oct. 16. The aircraft crashed near the village of Ulanov, Ukraine, killing Col. Petrenko Ivan Nikolaevichand, deputy commander of aviation – the chief of aviation air command “East” of the Air Forces of the Armed Forces of Ukraine, and Lt. Col. Seth “Jethro” Nehring, 194th Fighter Squadron pilot, flying in the backseat of the Su-27 Flanker.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2018-10-19
[TITLE]: Latest Video of Pair of Su-25M ultra-low altitude Low Pass
[METADATA/INTRO]: Latest Video of Pair of Su-25M ultra-low altitude Low Pass. Another Ukrainian Air Force Su-25 Insane Low Pass Video Emerges Ukrainian Air Force pilots fly low quite often and new videos showing a Ukrainian jet buzzing someone at air bases...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Latest Video of Pair of Su-25M ultra-low altitude Low Pass. Another Ukrainian Air Force Su-25 Insane Low Pass Video Emerges 
 Ukrainian Air Force pilots fly low quite often and new videos showing a Ukrainian jet buzzing someone at air bases around the country appear regularly. 
 The latest one is footage showing two Su-25M1 flying low over a taxiway. It’s not clear when it was filmed or where. However, considering the number of Il-76MD cargo aircraft that you can see in the clip, it could well be Melitopol airbase, in southeastern Ukraine, home of the 25th Transport Aviation Brigade. 
 The Su-25M1 Frogfoot jets were probably operating out of Mykolaiv/Kulbakino airbase, to the west of Melitopol, home of the 299th Tactical Aviation Brigade.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: Video Shows Ukrainian Fighter Pulling Off An Insanely-Low Flyby - Task & Purpose
[METADATA/INTRO]: The Ukrainian Air Force seems to be increasingly entertaining itself with breath-taking aerial acrobatics. Exhibit A came on Friday, when The Aviationist flagged an intense clip of an SU-25 Frogfoot buzzing Ukrainian military personnel on the ground. The footage emerged...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: - In June, another video captured another Su-24MR Fencer conducting another aggressive flyby across the Staro flight line. 
 - The recent incidents represent an uptick in recorded flybys after a relative lull in activity. In November 2016, footage showed a Su-27 Flanker nearly eat shit at an unidentified airbase after breaking right at the end of a runway, climbing directly over the head of an eerily-composed cameraman. 
 - In November 2015, a Su-25 Frogfoot pulled off a low-level victory aileron roll right above the heads of some female Ukrainian infantry posing for a photograph; the previous September, footage emerged of a massive Ukranian Il-76 cargo plane took a moment to buzz a fleet of parked Su-25s.
